businessSummary.oneLiner: Government and commercial AI data analytics platform leader with Gotham (defense), Foundry (enterprise), and AIP (AI platform)
businessSummary.description: Palantir builds AI data analytics platforms for government and enterprise. FY2025 revenue $4.48B (+60%). US commercial revenue topped $650M (Q4 +137% YoY). Pentagon adopted Maven AI as official program of record for all branches. Selected as Golden Dome $185B missile defense software developer. New Polymarket AI fraud prevention partnership expands into financial services. FY2026 guidance: $7.19B-$7.41B (+60-65%).
howTheyMakeMoney: Enterprise AI platform (Foundry) - enterprise data integration
AIP (AI Platform) - generative AI integration platform
AIP Bootcamps - rapid adoption programs for customer conversion

keyMetrics.name: Trailing P/E
keyMetrics.interpretation: vs S&P 500 IT avg 34x (7.4x premium). Motley Fool: 'needs 5x earnings growth (+489%) just to reach 50x P/E'

keyMetrics.name: Forward P/E (FY2026E)
keyMetrics.interpretation: Based on FY2026 EPS est. $1.22. Still 3x typical growth stock multiples of 30-40x

keyMetrics.name: Revenue Growth (FY2026E)
keyMetrics.interpretation: From $4.48B to $7.40B. AI defense demand driving accelerating growth

keyMetrics.name: P/S Ratio
keyMetrics.interpretation: vs software avg 10-15x. 5-7x premium. Still deeply overvalued relative to growth

keyMetrics.name: Market Cap
keyMetrics.interpretation: -31% from 52-week high of $207. Caught in broad tech selloff

keyMetrics.name: RSI
keyMetrics.interpretation: Neutral zone. Room before oversold but weak bounce momentum in downtrend

growthDrivers.title: Motley Fool: 'Palantir banked a blockbuster week' (3/29)
growthDrivers.description: Golden Dome, Maven program of record, and Polymarket partnership named as three blockbuster developments. Fundamentals continue strengthening

growthDrivers.title: Polymarket AI fraud prevention partnership (3/26)
growthDrivers.description: Palantir's Vergence AI monitors transactions and user behavior in real-time on Polymarket. Opens door to broader financial services AI surveillance

growthDrivers.title: Golden Dome $185B missile defense software selection (3/25)
growthDrivers.description: Palantir and Anduril developing software for Trump's $185B Golden Dome. Motley Fool: Phase 1 alone worth 'many billions'

growthDrivers.title: Czech National Bank purchases PLTR (3/26)
growthDrivers.description: Czech central bank bought 24,730 shares. A central bank buying an AI defense stock is a powerful institutional confidence signal

growthDrivers.title: Bain & Company global partnership expansion (3/26)
growthDrivers.description: Extended partnership with Bain to accelerate global AIP and Foundry deployment. Consulting channel for enterprise market penetration

growthDrivers.title: Maven AI official US military adoption (Program of Record, 3/23)
growthDrivers.description: Pentagon designated Maven Smart System as official program of record. Long-term deployment across all military branches confirmed

competitiveAdvantage.summary: 20+ years of government defense AI track record, top-level security clearances, Maven adoption across US military, combat-proven in Iran conflict. Moats money cannot buy

moats.description: Ontology platform deeply integrated into government/military systems. Replacement costs prohibitively high
Highest-level clearances and 20+ year trust relationships. Virtually impossible for new entrants to replicate
AIP Bootcamps drive rapid adoption. Ontology data model ecosystem expanding
Used in thousands of real-time strikes during Iran conflict. Battle data is the ultimate competitive advantage

competitors.name: Anduril Industries
competitors.detail: Defense AI/drone startup. Partnered with Palantir on Golden Dome. $60B valuation

competitors.name: Snowflake / Databricks
competitors.detail: Enterprise data platform competition. Far behind in government/defense access

competitors.name: Microsoft / AWS
competitors.detail: Cloud giants entering government AI. Cannot match Ontology's integration depth

competitors.name: C3.ai
competitors.detail: Enterprise AI platform. Significantly behind in both revenue and growth

recentNews.headline: Motley Fool: 'Palantir banked a blockbuster week, 3 things shareholders should know'
recentNews.significance: Golden Dome, Maven, Polymarket named blockbuster developments. Same outlet also published '2030 price will be about the same as today' citing P/E 250x requiring 5x earnings growth

recentNews.headline: Motley Fool: 'Prediction: PLTR stock price in 2030 will be about the same'
recentNews.significance: At P/E 250x, earnings need to grow 489% to reach P/E of 50x. Even at +65% annual revenue growth, valuation normalization takes 5 years

recentNews.headline: PLTR drops 3% to $143.06, tech selloff continues
recentNews.significance: Third consecutive decline. Below SMA50 ($149) and SMA200 ($169). Weekly -5.1%

recentNews.headline: Polymarket Vergence AI fraud prevention partnership
recentNews.significance: Palantir's Vergence AI handles real-time trade surveillance and fraud detection on Polymarket. Start of financial services expansion

recentNews.headline: Golden Dome $185B missile defense software selection
recentNews.significance: Phase 1 alone worth 'many billions.' Major long-term defense pipeline relative to FY2025 total revenue of $4.48B

analystOpinions.notableComment: Consensus $199 (+39%). Motley Fool acknowledges blockbuster week while simultaneously publishing bearish '5 years same price' thesis. P/E 250x requires 5x earnings growth to normalize

risks.title: Extreme valuation: '5 years same price' bear case
risks.description: P/E 250x (7.4x IT sector avg of 34x). Motley Fool: earnings must grow 489% to reach P/E 50x. Even +65% annual growth takes 5 years to normalize

risks.title: Below both 50-day and 200-day moving averages
risks.description: SMA50 ($149) and SMA200 ($169) both overhead. Three consecutive declines, weekly -5.1%. $126 support open

risks.title: Macro tech selloff risk
risks.description: Iran-Israel conflict, oil surge, No Kings protests. Most hostile environment for high-valuation tech

risks.title: Analyst opinion split
risks.description: 17 Buy, 14 Hold, 5 Sell. Market skepticism about valuation persists

risks.title: Stock-based compensation (SBC) dilution
risks.description: Share count growing 4.67% annually. Ongoing SBC dilutes existing shareholder value

buyReasons.title: Blockbuster week: Golden Dome + Maven + Polymarket
buyReasons.rationale: Golden Dome $185B (Phase 1 'many billions'), Maven official military adoption, Polymarket AI partnership. Three major developments in one week. Fundamentals at all-time strongest

buyReasons.title: Polymarket partnership opens financial services
buyReasons.rationale: Vergence AI fraud prevention for Polymarket is just the beginning. Potential to expand AI surveillance across financial services

buyReasons.title: -31% from highs, fundamentals stronger than ever
buyReasons.rationale: 52-week high $207 to $143. Fundamentals strengthened by Golden Dome, Maven, Polymarket while price declined

buyReasons.title: Trefis: 50%+ rally catalyst analysis
buyReasons.rationale: Past rally patterns match current catalysts (Golden Dome, Maven, commercial growth). Significant upside if sentiment turns

overallOpinion: Motley Fool called last week a 'blockbuster' citing Golden Dome, Maven, and Polymarket. The same outlet also published 'stock price in 2030 will be about the same as today.' P/E 250x demands 5x earnings growth (+489%) to normalize

The Polymarket Vergence AI partnership is a new growth vector. Starting with prediction market fraud prevention, it could expand into broader financial services surveillance. A concrete example of civilian AI application beyond defense
Below SMA50 ($149) and SMA200 ($169). Iran conflict and protests are crushing high-valuation tech. Valuation remains the core burden: P/S 76x is 5x software industry average
Consensus $199 (+39%). Down 31% from highs. Fundamentals are at all-time best but price needs time to catch up to earnings growth. Hold, small DCA positions only

investmentVerdict.summary: Blockbuster week (Golden Dome, Maven, Polymarket) makes fundamentals the strongest ever, but P/E 250x demands 5x earnings growth. Motley Fool's '5 years same price' thesis highlights the math. Macro headwinds and valuation burden work simultaneously. Small DCA possible but full conviction buying requires patience

threads.hook: PLTR: 'Blockbuster week' vs '5 years same price' coexist in contradiction
threads.text:
$PLTR $143.06 (-3%)

Motley Fool called last week a blockbuster: Golden Dome, Maven, Polymarket. Same outlet: 'stock price in 2030 will be about the same.' P/E 250x needs 5x earnings growth.

Polymarket Vergence AI is a new financial services vector. Czech central bank buying. Fundamentals at all-time best.

But P/S 76x blocks the way. Consensus $199 (+39%). Hold, small DCA only.

witty.hook: 'Blockbuster!' and 'Same price in 5 years.' Same outlet. Same day
witty.text:
$PLTR $143.06 (-3%)

Blockbuster week! (Motley Fool) 2030 same price. (Also Motley Fool) Published on the same day. P/E 250x creates its own paradox.

Golden Dome, Maven, Polymarket, central bank. The bullish case has never been stronger. Market: 'P/E 250x.' That's the whole bear case.

Earnings need 5x growth. At +65%/yr, 5 years. Consensus $199. Hold.

empathy.hook: PLTR shareholders, non-stop good news but the price keeps dropping
empathy.text:
$PLTR $143.06 (-3%), -31% from highs.

Golden Dome, Maven, Polymarket. Great news everywhere but the stock just falls. Incredibly frustrating.

Motley Fool calls it a blockbuster. But P/E 250x means earnings need time. The company is executing perfectly.

When macro noise fades, these fundamentals will shine. Consensus $199 (+39%). Small DCA. Long-term is the answer.
